feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: After the dust has settled, can we admit that it was kino?
entry.content: After the dust has settled, can we admit that it was kino?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: BUCK GUNN: Directors Now Avoiding DCU Work
entry.content: >DC’s biggest problem coming out of the Supergirl disaster isn’t the write-down. It’s the question underneath the entire slate: who is going to make these movies? >Word around town, per our Hollywood insiders, is that filmmakers are hesitant to take on big DC franchises. And that hesitancy has only hardened since Supergirl bombed, for two reasons our insiders spell out. >We Reported The DCU Director Problem Back In October >First, the timestamp. This isn’t post-flop pile-on. >Back in October, ou

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Bros, I think Half-Life 3’s been cancelled.
entry.content: The game simply doesn’t exist. Unlike GTA 6, which actually took 13 years and US$2 billion to develop, Half-Life 3 has simply been cancelled. The game doesn’t exist at all. No matter how much we pray to the god Gayben, a miracle won’t happen. Just give up.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >Rumors that Blizzard is making an actual, genuinely NEW, WarCraft RTS and even …
entry.content: >Rumors that Blizzard is making an actual, genuinely NEW, WarCraft RTS and even an Arthas ARPG https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NDo LHQzDHK4 I honestly don't know if that is true and that it's said the RTS will be called WarCraft Chronicles and it will be connected to WoW since Blizzard doesn't want to cut itself off from WoW. And this right here is the biggest mistake here if its all true. There are people that don't want any of this WoW shit. WoW ruined the lore and the setting. All they have t

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I'm too young to have a natural death right now
entry.content: It's not something I like. Some soul said," you might get reincarnated as a blond and blue-eyed prince but you are doomed to live your whole life as a demon lord's possession if you die by killing yourself." I'm afraid, I don't want to be a possession, I want to be free. So, will I have to wait for my death?

entry.content: >Extra Thick Sausage Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. entry.title: /b/ Random: 8=>
/g/ Technology: He stood unshaken, a voice in the storm A man of conviction, a heart reborn He s…
entry.content: He stood unshaken, a voice in the storm A man of conviction, a heart reborn He spoke the truth when the cost was high He lived for GNU, unafraid to die We are Richard Stallman, we carry the flame We'll fight for the Software, we'll honor his name We are Richard Stallman, his courage our own Together unbroken, we'll make Linux known

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: 'Hancock' original script, then called 'Tonight, He Comes'
entry.content: >John Hancock is a nihilistic, depressed, alcoholic superhuman who lives in New York City. Due to his powers, Hancock believes that it's his obligation to protect the innocent, even though he hates having the weight of the world in his shoulders, and attempts to dull the pain by indulging in drugs and prostitutes. >After foiling a robbery, Hancock befriends bank teller Mary Longfellow, who is estranged from her husband Horus, a cowardly security guard. Horus and Mary's sex life is dull, and Horu

entry.title: /g/ Technology: A man named Thom Henderson wrote a compression program called ARC...
entry.content: ... back in the 1980s. He distributed his code widely as shareware, along with source - because how else would people be able to use it and adjust it for their machines? - but directly said he "did it for the mail". Then a guy named Phil Katz (yes, he is) took the source code, threw assembly into the speed-critical parts (thus tying it to x86) and released it as paid software (thus violating ARC's license) called PKARC. Henderson heard about it, and called in independent software expert John Nav

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I'm shitposting on 4chan while my AI agent does work for me. I've built my own S…
entry.content: I'm shitposting on 4chan while my AI agent does work for me. I've built my own SaaS that looks wonderful and works better than anything else I've worked on for other businesses in my career. Everything is streamlined and anyone can feel like a boss now. So why do I still feel so blackpilled?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Have le-TikTok-disease of ADHD and am slowly becoming mentally retarded due to c…
entry.content: Have le-TikTok-disease of ADHD and am slowly becoming mentally retarded due to corporate AI agentic usage. Used to like learning about functional programming and Lisp, make cool abstractions that improve codebase maintainability, learn about best practices and actually solving problems the right way and whatnot. Now after 5 months of the "AI special" in an undisclosed company, I can barely even explain what the code I'm "writing" does anymore, the codebase is fucking slop and I'm never reading t

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >use perplexity to find good cdn for global userscript I'm working on >gives me …
entry.content: >use perplexity to find good cdn for global userscript I'm working on >gives me a link to maxcdn >company shut down years ago >was once a valid link >domain was purchased by someone else and the cdn urls were replaced with obfuscated cookie stealer >have to change all of my passwords to reset cookies I think this is the point where I will stop even trying to attempt using AI for anything.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Two catchy weirdos, eighteen songs, and the hits just keep on coming in an exube…
entry.content: Two catchy weirdos, eighteen songs, and the hits just keep on coming in an exuberantly annoying show of creative superabundance. Their secret is that as unmediated pop postmodernists they can be themselves stealing from anywhere, modulating without strain or personal commitment from hick to nut to nerd. Like the cross-eyed bear in the regretful but not altogether kind "Hide Away Folk Family," their "shoes are laced with irony," but that doesn't doom them to art-school cleverness or never meaning

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Freaks and Geeks thread
entry.content: Let's discuss the show Freaks and Geeks.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Remember that episode where Morty asks Rick to make something to make Jessica fa…
entry.content: Remember that episode where Morty asks Rick to make something to make Jessica fall in love with him? So then he does and Jessica becomes obsessed with Morty but it ruins everything? And it’s this dissection of the love potion trope and how it’s basically rape since it removes someone’s agency and it reveals how a seemingly innocent wish belies Morty’s lack of character? They should make a movie like that!

entry.title: /g/ Technology: /g/uys I really dont feel so good about this
entry.content: How do we fix this? Is this fixable? Will these prices go down? What are the implications of everything 3x in 3 years? Will these be 4x next year? Is this sustainable? I sense a massive tech problem, but I see no one is concerned (IRL). I have never lived in a time period like this. My entire life, tech has only gone one direction: cheaper and more accessible as newer and better tech replaces the old. There is nothing new. There is only old. Old is getting more expensive AND FAST! Like hyperinfl

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What do you guys think of Bluefin and immutable distros in general? I recently t…
entry.content: What do you guys think of Bluefin and immutable distros in general? I recently tried it and I think it should absolutely be the defacto normgroid recommendation. Much better choice than stuff like Ubuntu and Mint for non-tech people.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Let's face it, you only like Linux because you like the "idea" of an open-source…
entry.content: Let's face it, you only like Linux because you like the "idea" of an open-source OS. If you fairly compared it against Windows on features, UI, UX, reliability, compatibility (backwards, forwards and between systems), not relying on centralized repositories, ease of developing and distributing programs for it, etc. it would lose on every metric.

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, >“Wh…
entry.content: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, >“Who do people say the Son of Man is?” They replied, >“Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.” > “But what about you?” he asked. > “Who do you say I am?” Your fortune: （ ´_ゝ`）ﾌｰﾝ

entry.title: /g/ Technology: do 4chan cookies expire for inactivity? i stopped using my secondary laptop with…
entry.content: do 4chan cookies expire for inactivity? i stopped using my secondary laptop with 1 captcha cookie for a week today i wanted to post something and for my surprise i had to wait 60 seconds just to get 4 captchas again + ip blocked for abuse, so i verified my email now i'm doing 3 captchas per post same shit happened to the pc i have at my parents' house, but this time i just imported the cookies from my main laptop with 1 captcha cookie and it got fixed

entry.title: /int/ International: >The April 2011 Fukushima earthquake struck while Endo, then 10 years old, was i…
entry.content: >The April 2011 Fukushima earthquake struck while Endo, then 10 years old, was in class and heavily damaged her school. Her family and their homes survived, and they were not required to evacuate from the subsequent release of radiation at the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant; as she wasn't allowed to go outside, she continued to practice football inside her home, which contributed to her ability to handle the ball in confined spaces. >In a 2023 interview, Endo said that at the time she had

entry.content: What can you expect from filthy little heathens? Their whole disgusting race is like a curse Their skin's a hellish red They're only good when dead They're vermin, as I said, and worse They're savages, savages Barely even human Savages, savages Drive them from our shore They're not like you and me Which means they must be evil We must sound the drums of war They're savages, savages Dirty shrieking devils Now we sound the drums of war
